title: 🧑‍💻 Why Developers Should Care About Typing Speed (It’s Not Just About Speed)
content:
As developers, we optimize everything:

Code performance
System design
Algorithms

But we ignore one thing we use all day:

👉 Typing

⚡ The Hidden Bottleneck

Think about it:

Writing code
Debugging
ChatGPT prompts 😄
Documentation

Typing speed directly affects your output.

Even a 20% improvement can save hours every week.

🧪 Quick Math

Let’s say:

You type ~2000 words/day
At 40 WPM → ~50 minutes
At 80 WPM → ~25 minutes

👉 You save 25 minutes/day

That’s:
➡️ ~12.5 hours/month
➡️ ~150 hours/year

🧠 It’s Not About Speed Alone

The real benefits:

1. Better Flow State

Less interruption → deeper focus

2. Fewer Mistakes

Higher accuracy = less debugging friction

3. Faster Execution

Ideas → code instantly

🛠️ Tools That Actually Help

Most typing tools are:

Cluttered
Gamified too much
Not practical

I found something cleaner:

👉 https://orangetype.in

What I liked:

Minimal UI
Real-time stats
Good for consistent practice
📈 How to Improve (Developer Style)
1. Treat It Like a Skill
Track metrics
Optimize weaknesses
Iterate
2. Focus on Accuracy First

Speed comes naturally after.

3. Practice With Real Content

Code-like text, not random quotes.

📚 Want a Structured Plan?

I found a detailed breakdown here:

👉 https://blogs.orangetype.in

It covers:

Practice routines
Common mistakes
Speed-building strategy
🔥 Final Thought

We spend hours optimizing systems…

But ignore the interface between brain → keyboard.

Fix that, and everything becomes faster.

💬 What’s your typing speed right now?
